clusters.root_cause_summary: The upstair centroid in both scenes lies in a navmesh-disconnected component, making the Phase 1 stair waypoint geometrically unreachable. No streak-based disable or redirect mechanism has produced any observable behavioral change (byte-identical fingerprints across all candidates), because the Phase 1 window is too short and/or the disconnected region is larger than any BFS snap or CC-validated carrot has bridged.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: IDENTICAL: upstair centroid is off-navmesh; Reach_stair_centroid: False throughout Phase 1; no floor transition is achieved without an explicit carrot mechanism placing the Phase 2 waypoint on the robot's navigable component. DIFFERENT: qyAac8rV8Zk was fully solved by T6 candidate_0 (0.4m pullback + centroid bypass at paused=8→Phase 2) proving the mechanism works when the carrot lands in the robot's component — candidate_1's 0.6m projection immediately reinstated disconnection (min_dis_to_downstair oscillates 160–166, DTG=2.075m). q3zU7Yy5E5s has a Phase 1 window of only 12 steps and centroid at [-2.12, 3.28]; every pullback variant tested has been a behavioral no-op, indicating the disconnected region fully encloses the stair bounding box so no radial offset reaches the navigable component.
clusters.transfer_notes: qyAac8rV8Zk's T6 candidate_0 success proves that centroid bypass + 0.4m carrot pullback is sufficient and fragile: reverting 0.6m→0.4m is an immediate, zero-risk fix for that scene. For q3zU7Yy5E5s, the Phase 1 window (12 steps) is categorically too short for any streak-count trigger; the only untested lever that bypasses this window entirely is a navmesh connectivity pre-check at Phase 1 entry — if the upstair centroid pixel is not in the navigable map, immediately redirect to the downstair centroid without attempting any Phase 1 approach. This skips Phase 1 for q3zU7Yy5E5s while being a no-op for scenes where the centroid is reachable.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: Two-part cluster fix: (1) restore DP9 0.4m carrot pullback as sole carrot mechanism (drop 0.6m projection) to recover qyAac8rV8Zk — already proven in T6 candidate_0; (2) navmesh_connectivity_check_at_Phase1_entry: at gcts_step=0, if upstair centroid pixel is not set in navigable_map, immediately invoke downstair redirect (force climb_direction=2) without entering any Phase 1 GCTS loop — this is the only mechanism that bypasses the 12-step Phase 1 window for q3zU7Yy5E5s entirely.

clusters.root_cause_summary: Candidate_0 (baseline) reached DTG=0.131m at step 499 but triggered a false-positive STOP during initialize mode (floor_step=1 after the second floor switch), preventing goal confirmation. Candidate_1 introduced a compounding regression: its 0.6m forward projection produced zero paused_step resets during Phase 2 stair traversal (vs three in candidate_0), exiting at a frontier-poor second-floor position and degrading DTG from 0.131m to 0.683m.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. The binding failure in candidate_0 is a should_stop return during initialize mode at step 499 when the agent is within 0.131m DTG but floor_step=1 (floor not yet explored, no frontier established). Candidate_1's 0.6m forward projection is a distinct regression layered on top: zero paused_step resets → shallow traversal → no second-floor frontiers → episode ends at step 257. Both failures must be addressed: revert to 0.4m pullback to restore the frontier-rich traversal, then suppress the false-positive STOP during initialize mode.
clusters.transfer_notes: The candidate_0 vs candidate_1 contrast isolates the two failure modes cleanly: the 0.6m projection regression is upstream (frontier supply), and the false-positive stop is downstream (goal confirmation). Reverting to 0.4m pullback is a prerequisite because it restores the three paused_step resets and 280+ explore steps seen in candidate_0. Once the agent can reach near-goal DTG again, the stop guard (suppress STOP when mode==initialize AND floor_step<20) is the remaining fix — this guard is a no-op in all other scenes where the agent does not stop in initialize mode near the goal.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: suppress_STOP_when_mode_is_initialize_and_floor_step_lt_20: in should_stop (or its hook), gate the STOP action on floor_step>=20 when mode==initialize — this directly prevents the false-positive at step 499 DTG=0.131m. Must be paired with restoring DP9 0.4m carrot pullback (drop 0.6m projection) to recover the frontier-rich Phase 2 traversal that candidate_1 destroyed.

forbidden_moves.reason: Candidate_1 confirmed: 0.6m projection moves the Phase 2 carrot back into the disconnected navmesh region in qyAac8rV8Zk (min_dis_to_downstair oscillates 160–166, Reach_stair_centroid False throughout, DTG=2.075m vs 0.094m in candidate_0). Globally forbidden per NOTE.
Caused the candidate_1 regression in XB4GS9ShBRE: zero paused_step resets (vs three in candidate_0) → zero second-floor frontiers → DTG degraded from 0.131m to 0.683m. Globally forbidden per NOTE.

All threshold variants tested (N=8, 10, 30) produced byte-identical behavioral fingerprints for q3zU7Yy5E5s; Phase 1 window is 12 steps and sporadic True ticks reset the streak counter, making it impossible to reach any meaningful threshold within the window.

Behavioral fingerprint identical to DP9 0.4m pullback (T5 candidate_10 confirmed for both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk); for q3zU7Yy5E5s the entire stair bounding box is disconnected so CC validation finds no valid point and falls back to the disconnected centroid.

Ruled out for q3zU7Yy5E5s: the disconnected region fully encloses the stair bounding box; BFS flood-fill finds no navigable pixel within the box and the snap falls back to the original disconnected centroid, producing a behavioral no-op.
Globally forbidden per NOTE. These scenes fail during Phase 1 approach (centroid unreachable), not during stair climb success evaluation.
Globally forbidden per NOTE. XB4GS9ShBRE's binding failure is a false-positive STOP in should_stop during initialize mode at DTG=0.131m — a different code path from the stair climb success gate.
